words.definition: a person who has come to live permanently in a different country from the one they were born in
examples: immigrants from other European countries
immigrants to the United States
Immigrants and their descendants have played a vital role in building this nation.
The president vowed to continue deporting illegal immigrants.
The program protects undocumented immigrants who came to the US as children.

words.definition:
the process of coming to live permanently in a different country from the one you were born in; the number of people who do this

the place at a port, an airport, etc. where the passports and other documents of people coming into a country are checked
examples: legal/illegal immigration
We are strongly supportive of immigration and multiculturalism.
laws restricting immigration into the US
There was an increase in immigration from Eastern Europe.
immigration policy/reform

words.definition: likely to happen very soon
examples: the imminent threat of invasion
The system is in imminent danger of collapse.
An announcement about his resignation is imminent.
An attack seems imminent.

words.definition:
that cannot catch or be affected by a particular disease or illness

not affected by something, especially something that you might expect to be harmful

protected from something and therefore able to avoid it
examples: Adults are often immune to German measles.
You'll eventually become immune to criticism.
Few people are immune to her charms.

words.definition:
the powerful effect that something has on somebody/something

the act of one object hitting another; the force with which this happens
examples: a positive/a negative/an adverse impact
a significant/major/huge impact
to have/make an impact
to reduce/minimize the impact of something
the environmental impact of tourism

words.definition:
annoyed by somebody/something, especially because you have to wait for a long time

wanting to do something soon; wanting something to happen soon

unable or unwilling to accept something unpleasant
examples: I'd been waiting for twenty minutes and I was getting impatient.
The children were growing impatient.
Try not to be too impatient with her.
Sarah was becoming increasingly impatient at their lack of interest.
An impatient driver behind me sounded his horn.

words.definition: to make something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used
examples: to implement changes/decisions/policies/reforms
A new work programme for young people will be implemented.

words.definition: the act of making something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used
examples: the implementation of the new system

words.definition:
a possible effect or result of an action or a decision

something that is suggested or indirectly stated (= something that is implied)

the fact of being involved, or of involving somebody, in something, especially a crime
examples: They failed to consider the wider implications of their actions.
The development of the site will have implications for the surrounding countryside.

words.definition:
to suggest that something is true or that you feel or think something, without saying so directly

to make it seem likely that something is true or exists

to make something necessary in order to be successful
examples: Are you implying (that) I am wrong?
His silence seemed to imply agreement.
High-yield bonds, as the name implies, offer a high rate of interest.

words.definition:
a product or service that is brought into one country from another

the act of bringing a product or service into one country from another

importance

the meaning of something, especially when it is not immediately clear
examples: food imports from abroad
cheap imports into the United States
The report calls for a ban on the import of hazardous waste.
imports of oil
import duties/tariffs

words.definition: the quality of being important
examples: She stressed the importance of careful preparation.
to emphasize/highlight the importance of something
He understands the importance of getting the details right.
They attach great importance to the project.
the relative importance of the two ideas

words.definition:
having a great effect on people or things; of great value

having great influence or authority
examples: an important issue/question/point/factor
We have an important decision to make.
Money played an important role in his life.
Listening is an important part of the job.
one of the most important collections of American art

words.definition:
to introduce a new law, rule, tax, etc.; to order that a rule, punishment, etc. be used

to force somebody/something to have to deal with something that is difficult or unpleasant

to make somebody accept the same opinions, wishes, etc. as your own

to expect somebody to do something for you or to spend time with you, when it may not be convenient for them

to make somebody/something accept or be aware of your presence or ideas
examples: The UN Security Council imposed sanctions in 1992.
to impose a penalty/fine/sentence/ban
A new tax was imposed on fuel.
He described the limitations imposed by his disease.
This system imposes additional financial burdens on many people.

words.definition:
that cannot exist or be done; not possible

very difficult to deal with

a thing that is or seems impossible

a difficult or impossible task
examples: almost/virtually/nearly impossible
Only a year ago this would have seemed impossible.
Getting any kind of decent job was proving impossible.
an impossible dream/goal
Going downstairs would be impossible for him with his sprained ankle.

words.definition:
if a person or thing impresses you, you admire them or it

to make somebody understand how important, serious, etc. something is by emphasizing it

to have a great effect on something, especially somebody’s mind, imagination, etc.
examples: We interviewed a number of candidates but none of them impressed us.
The Grand Canyon never fails to impress people.
The Grand Canyon never fails to impress.

He impressed her with his sincerity.

words.definition: admiring somebody/something because you think they are particularly good, interesting, etc.
examples: I must admit I am impressed.
We were all impressed by her enthusiasm.
He was particularly impressed by her essay.
She was suitably impressed (= as impressed as somebody had hoped) with the painting.

words.definition:
an idea, a feeling or an opinion that you get about somebody/something, or that somebody/something gives you

the effect that an experience or a person has on somebody/something

a drawing showing what a person looks like or what a place or a building will look like in the future

a clever or funny copy of the way a person acts or speaks

an appearance that may be false
examples: My first impression of him was favourable.
She gives the impression of being very busy.

words.definition: making you admire them, because they are very large, good, skilful, etc.
examples: an impressive performance
an impressive array/list/collection

words.definition: to put somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape

words.definition: the act of putting somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape; the state of being there

words.definition: to become better than before; to make something/somebody better than before

words.definition:
the act of making something better; the process of something becoming better

a change in something that makes it better; something that is better than it was before
examples: The economy has shown significant improvement over the past 9 months.
We expect to see further improvement over the coming year.
Sales figures continue to show signs of improvement.

words.definition:
at a point within an area or a space

within the shape of something; surrounded by something

into something

forming the whole or part of something/somebody; contained within something/somebody

during a period of time
examples: a country in Africa
The kids were playing in the street.
It's in that drawer.
I read about it in the paper.
She was lying in bed.

words.definition: the fact of not being able to do something

words.definition:
not enough; not good enough

not able, or not confident enough, to deal with a situation

words.definition: not suitable or appropriate in a particular situation

words.definition:
something that encourages you to do something

a payment or concession (= a reduction in the amount of money that has to be paid) that encourages somebody to do something

words.definition:
a unit for measuring length or height, equal to 2.54 centimetres. There are 12 inches in a foot.

a small amount or distance

the whole of something

completely

used to say that if you allow some people a small amount of freedom or power they will see you as weak and try to take a lot more
examples: She's a few inches taller than me.
a laptop with a 15-inch screen

words.definition:
the extent to which something happens or has an effect

the way in which light meets a surface

the angle of incidence

words.definition:
something that happens, especially something unusual or unpleasant

a serious or violent event, such as a crime, an accident or an attack

a disagreement between two countries, often involving military forces

words.definition:
wanting to do something

tending to do something; likely to do something

having a natural ability for something; preferring to do something

sloping; at an angle
examples: She was inclined to trust him.

words.definition:

words.definition:
the fact of including somebody/something; the fact of being included

words.definition: the money that a person, a region, a country, etc. earns from work, from investing money, from business, etc.

words.definition:
to include something so that it forms a part of something

to create a legally recognized company
examples: The new car design incorporates all the latest safety features.

words.definition:
not accurate or true

speaking or behaving in a way that does not follow the accepted standards or rules

words.definition: to become greater in amount, number, value, etc.; to make something greater in amount, number, value, etc.

words.definition: more and more all the time

words.definition:

extremely good

words.definition:
extremely

in a way that is very difficult to believe

incredibly stupid/boring
incredibly powerful/strong
incredibly lucky/talented/beautiful

words.definition:
if you incur something unpleasant, you are in a situation in which you have to deal with it

if you incur costs, you have to pay them

words.definition:
used after very and an adjective or adverb to emphasize a statement, description, etc.

used to emphasize a positive statement or answer

used to add information to a statement

used to show that you are surprised at something or that you find something silly

used when you are repeating a question that somebody has just asked and showing that you do not know the answer
examples: Thank you very much indeed!

words.definition:
freedom from political control by other countries

the time when a country gains freedom from political control by another country

the freedom to organize your own life, make your own decisions, etc. without needing help from other people
examples: Argentina gained independence from Spain in 1816.
independence celebrations
the first elections since independence

words.definition:
having its own government

confident and free to do things without needing help from other people

having or earning enough money so that you do not have to rely on somebody else for help

done or given by somebody who is not involved in a situation and so is able to judge it fairly

not connected with or influenced by something; not connected with each other
examples: an independent state/nation/country

The country became fully independent from France in 1960.

words.definition:
a list of names or topics that are referred to in a book, etc., usually arranged at the end of a book in alphabetical order or listed in a separate file or book

a box of cards with information on them, arranged in alphabetical order

a system that shows the level of prices and wages, etc. so that they can be compared with those of a previous date

a number giving a measurement of something compared with a particular standard

a sign or measure that something else can be judged by

words.definition:
to show that something is true or exists

to be a sign of something; to show that something is possible or likely

to mention something, especially in an indirect way

to make somebody notice somebody/something, especially by pointing or moving your head

to represent information without using words

words.definition:
a sign that shows you what something is like or how a situation is changing

words.definition:
a sign that a system, society, etc. is very bad or very wrong

an official statement accusing somebody of a crime

the act of officially accusing somebody of a crime
examples: The poverty in our cities is a damning indictment of modern society.

words.definition:
coming from a particular place and having lived there for a long time before other people came there; relating to, belonging to or developed by these people

having originally developed in a particular place, rather than having been brought there from somewhere else
examples: the indigenous peoples/languages of an area

indigenous land/territory

words.definition:

not done directly; done through somebody/something else

avoiding saying something in a clear and obvious way

not going in a straight line

words.definition:
a person considered separately rather than as part of a group

a single member of a class of things

a person who is original and very different from others

a person of a particular type, especially a strange one
examples: The competition is open to both teams and individuals.

words.definition:
located, done or used inside a building

relating to sports played indoors
examples: an indoor swimming pool

the world indoor 200 metres champion

words.definition: inside or into a building
examples: I prefer to stay indoors in this really hot weather.

words.definition:
to persuade or influence somebody to do something

to cause something

to make a woman start giving birth to her baby by giving her special drugs; to make a baby start being born by giving the mother special drugs
examples: Nothing would induce me to take the job.

words.definition:

to satisfy a particular desire, interest, etc.

to be too generous in allowing somebody to have or do whatever they like

to take part in an activity, especially one that is illegal
examples: They went into town to indulge in some serious shopping.

words.definition:
connected with industry

used by industries

having many industries

very great in extent or amount
examples: an industrial dispute

Industrial production fell in December by 1.4 per cent.
policy with regard to future industrial development

words.definition:
the production of goods from raw materials, especially in factories

the people and activities involved in producing a particular thing, or in providing a particular service

the quality of working hard
examples: the needs of British industry
the Department of Trade and Industry
She got a job in industry.
the steel/oil/auto industry
the music/film/tourism industry

words.definition: the unfair difference between groups of people in society, when some have more wealth, status or opportunities than others
examples: We need to tackle inequality of opportunity wherever we find it.

words.definition:
that you cannot avoid or prevent

happening so often that you always expect it

something that is certain to happen
examples: It was an inevitable consequence of the decision.

words.definition:
as is certain to happen

as you would expect

words.definition: well known for being bad or evil

words.definition:
a baby or very young child

a child at school between the ages of four and seven
examples: a nursery for infants under two

words.definition:
to make a disease or an illness spread to a person, an animal or a plant

to make a substance contain harmful bacteria that can spread disease

to make a computer virus spread to another computer or program

to make somebody share a particular feeling
examples: It is not possible to infect another person through kissing.

words.definition:
the act or process of causing or getting a disease

an illness that is caused by bacteria or a virus and that affects one part of the body
examples: to cause/prevent infection

words.definition:
to reach an opinion or decide that something is true on the basis of information that is available

words.definition:
a fall in the value of money and a general increase in prices; the rate at which this happens

a general rise in the level of something that is awarded

the act or process of filling something with air or gas
examples: the fight against rising inflation

a high/low rate of inflation

words.definition: to make somebody/something suffer something unpleasant
examples: They inflicted a humiliating defeat on the home team.

words.definition:
the effect that somebody/something has on the way a person thinks or behaves or on the way that something works or develops

words.definition: having a lot of influence on somebody/something
